file.content:
---
name: FrameLog implementation status
description: Full implementation status of the FRAME//LOG Android app — data, VM, and UI layers
type: project
---

## Fully complete layers (compiling cleanly as of 2026-03-19)

**Database / data layer:**
- All 12 Room entities in `data/db/entity/`
- All enums in `Enums.kt`: CameraBodyFormat, FilmFormat, ShutterIncrements, ApertureIncrements, ColorType, RollStatus
- TypeConverters for all enums (string values, not ordinals)
- All 11 DAOs in `data/db/dao/`
- Relation types in `data/db/relation/`: RollWithDetails, FrameWithDetails, KitWithDetails, + join relations
- Export types in `RollExport.kt`
- AppDatabase: singleton, all DAO accessors, 3 multi-DAO @Transaction methods
- All 4 repositories in `data/repository/`

**Infrastructure:**
- `data/AppPreferences.kt` — SharedPreferences wrapper (activeRollId, onboarding, settings, per-roll currentFrame + MRU filters)
- `ui/util/ExposureValues.kt` — shutter speed + aperture value lists, display formatters, EC values
- `ui/util/ExportFormatter.kt` — CSV, JSON, plain text formatters for RollExport

**ViewModel layer (13 VMs):**
GearLibraryViewModel, LensDetailViewModel, CameraBodyDetailViewModel, FilterDetailViewModel,
FilmStockDetailViewModel, KitDetailViewModel, KitSelectorViewModel, RollListViewModel,
RollSetupViewModel, RollJournalViewModel, FrameDetailViewModel, QuickScreenViewModel, SettingsViewModel

---

## UI layer — partially started (session 2026-03-19 evening, interrupted)

**Done — compiling status unknown (not yet verified):**
- `ui/navigation/NavRoutes.kt` — all type-safe @Serializable route definitions (Navigation Compose 2.8.0)
- `ui/navigation/NavGraph.kt` — FrameLogNavGraph NavHost; Gear Library routes wired, others commented as TODO
- `MainActivity.kt` — updated to use FrameLogNavGraph + rememberNavController (placeholder removed)
- `ui/gear/GearLibraryScreen.kt` — full 5-tab implementation: ScrollableTabRow, per-tab search + sort dropdown + LazyColumn, gear cards (LensCard, BodyCard, FilterCard, FilmStockCard, KitCard), SearchAndSortRow generic composable, EmptyState, display name extensions
- `ui/gear/LensDetailScreen.kt` — full form: name, make, focalLength, mountType (autocomplete MountTypeField), maxAperture, minAperture, apertureIncrements (EnumDropdown), filterSize, notes; DiscardChangesDialog, DeleteConfirmDialog; also defines shared internal composables **MountTypeField** and **EnumDropdown** (used by all gear detail screens)

**Not yet started (next session, needs user go-ahead first):**
- `ui/gear/CameraBodyDetailScreen.kt`
- `ui/gear/FilterDetailScreen.kt`
- `ui/gear/FilmStockDetailScreen.kt`
- `ui/gear/KitDetailScreen.kt`
- All Roll screens (RollListScreen, RollSetupScreen, KitSelectorScreen, RollJournalScreen)
- FrameDetailScreen, QuickScreen, SettingsScreen, Onboarding screens

---

## Architecture notes

- No DI framework — repositories get AppDatabase passed directly
- Navigation: type-safe routes via @Serializable data classes; SavedStateHandle auto-populated with route params by Navigation 2.8.0
- ViewModel creation: `viewModel()` inside `composable<T>` — Navigation Compose handles Application + SavedStateHandle injection automatically
- Start destination is GearLibrary temporarily; will move to QuickScreen with onboarding/launch logic later
- MediumFormat excluded from all v1.0 UI (CameraBodyFormat, FilmFormat)
- `collectAsStateWithLifecycle()` used in screen composables (requires lifecycle-runtime-compose; available via lifecycle 2.8.0 BOM)

**Why:** App is offline-first, no server. data/model/ dir not created — entities serve as models directly.
**How to apply:** Wait for explicit go-ahead from user before starting large implementation blocks. Repository layer is the correct access point for ViewModels.

content.text:
Here's what was done on 2026-03-19:

**Data & infrastructure layer (completed earlier, verified compiling cleanly):**
- All 12 Room entities, 11 DAOs, 4 repositories
- Relation types, export types, AppDatabase singleton with transaction methods
- `AppPreferences.kt`, `ExposureValues.kt`, `ExportFormatter.kt`
- All 13 ViewModels

**UI layer (started in the evening, session was interrupted before verifying compile):**
- `NavRoutes.kt` — type-safe `@Serializable` route definitions
- `NavGraph.kt` — `FrameLogNavGraph` with Gear Library routes wired; all others commented as TODO
- `MainActivity.kt` — updated to use the real nav graph
- `GearLibraryScreen.kt` — full 5-tab implementation with search, sort, `LazyColumn`, and gear cards for all entity types
- `LensDetailScreen.kt` — full form with `MountTypeField` (autocomplete), `EnumDropdown`, discard/delete dialogs; these two composables are shared by the other gear detail screens

**Left off:** The session was interrupted before the compile status of the UI files was verified. The remaining gear detail screens (`CameraBodyDetailScreen`, `FilterDetailScreen`, `FilmStockDetailScreen`, `KitDetailScreen`) and all roll/frame/quick screens haven't been started yet.

Want to pick up where we left off?

1→package com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.navigation
     2→
     3→import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
     4→import androidx.navigation.NavHostController
     5→import androidx.navigation.compose.NavHost
     6→import androidx.navigation.compose.composable
     7→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.gear.CameraBodyDetailScreen
     8→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.gear.FilmStockDetailScreen
     9→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.gear.FilterDetailScreen
    10→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.gear.GearLibraryScreen
    11→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.gear.KitDetailScreen
    12→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.gear.LensDetailScreen
    13→
    14→/**
    15→ * Root navigation graph for FRAME//LOG.
    16→ *
    17→ * Temporary start destination is [GearLibrary] while the Gear Library screens are being
    18→ * built out. This will change to [QuickScreen] once the full app launch sequence is
    19→ * implemented (onboarding check → loaded roll check → Quick Screen or empty state).
    20→ *
    21→ * Each composable block creates its own ViewModel via viewModel(). Navigation Compose 2.8.0
    22→ * automatically populates SavedStateHandle with the typed route parameters, so ViewModels
    23→ * that read savedState["id"] receive the correct value from the route.
    24→ */
    25→@Composable
    26→fun FrameLogNavGraph(navController: NavHostController) {
    27→    NavHost(
    28→        navController = navController,
    29→        startDestination = GearLibrary,
    30→    ) {
    31→
    32→        // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    33→        // Gear Library
    34→        // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    35→
    36→        composable<GearLibrary> {
    37→            GearLibraryScreen(navController = navController)
    38→        }
    39→
    40→        composable<LensDetail> {
    41→            LensDetailScreen(navController = navController)
    42→        }
    43→
    44→        composable<CameraBodyDetail> {
    45→            CameraBodyDetailScreen(navController = navController)
    46→        }
    47→
    48→        composable<FilterDetail> {
    49→            FilterDetailScreen(navController = navController)
    50→        }
    51→
    52→        composable<FilmStockDetail> {
    53→            FilmStockDetailScreen(navController = navController)
    54→        }
    55→
    56→        composable<KitDetail> {
    57→            KitDetailScreen(navController = navController)
    58→        }
    59→
    60→        // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    61→        // Rolls — TODO: implement in next session
    62→        // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    63→
    64→        // composable<RollList> { RollListScreen(navController) }
    65→        // composable<RollSetup> { RollSetupScreen(navController) }
    66→        // composable<KitSelector> { KitSelectorScreen(navController) }
    67→        // composable<RollJournal> { RollJournalScreen(navController) }
    68→        // composable<FrameDetail> { FrameDetailScreen(navController) }
    69→
    70→        // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    71→        // Other root screens — TODO: implement in next session
    72→        // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    73→
    74→        // composable<QuickScreen> { QuickScreen(navController) }
    75→        // composable<Settings> { SettingsScreen(navController) }
    76→    }
    77→}
    78→

content.text: Let me read `LensDetailScreen.kt` and the relevant ViewModels to understand the pattern before writing the stubs.

content.content:
1→package com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.gear
     2→
     3→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Box
     4→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Column
     5→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxSize
     6→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxWidth
     7→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.padding
     8→import androidx.compose.foundation.rememberScrollState
     9→import androidx.compose.foundation.text.KeyboardOptions
    10→import androidx.compose.foundation.verticalScroll
    11→import androidx.compose.material.icons.Icons
    12→import androidx.compose.material.icons.automirrored.filled.ArrowBack
    13→import androidx.compose.material.icons.filled.Delete
    14→import androidx.compose.material3.AlertDialog
    15→import androidx.compose.material3.CircularProgressIndicator
    16→import androidx.compose.material3.DropdownMenuItem
    17→import androidx.compose.material3.ExperimentalMaterial3Api
    18→import androidx.compose.material3.ExposedDropdownMenuBox
    19→import androidx.compose.material3.ExposedDropdownMenuDefaults
    20→import androidx.compose.material3.Icon
    21→import androidx.compose.material3.IconButton
    22→import androidx.compose.material3.MenuAnchorType
    23→import androidx.compose.material3.OutlinedTextField
    24→import androidx.compose.material3.Scaffold
    25→import androidx.compose.material3.Text
    26→import androidx.compose.material3.TextButton
    27→import androidx.compose.material3.TopAppBar
    28→import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
    29→import androidx.compose.runtime.LaunchedEffect
    30→import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue
    31→import androidx.compose.runtime.mutableStateOf
    32→import androidx.compose.runtime.remember
    33→import androidx.compose.runtime.setValue
    34→import androidx.compose.ui.Alignment
    35→import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
    36→import androidx.compose.ui.text.input.KeyboardType
    37→import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
    38→import androidx.lifecycle.compose.collectAsStateWithLifecycle
    39→import androidx.lifecycle.viewmodel.compose.viewModel
    40→import androidx.navigation.NavController
    41→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.ApertureIncrements
    42→
    43→@OptIn(ExperimentalMaterial3Api::class)
    44→@Composable
    45→fun LensDetailScreen(
    46→    navController: NavController,
    47→    vm: LensDetailViewModel = viewModel(),
    48→) {
    49→    val state by vm.state.collectAsStateWithLifecycle()
    50→
    51→    var showDiscardDialog by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
    52→    var showDeleteDialog by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
    53→
    54→    LaunchedEffect(Unit) {
    55→        vm.events.collect { event ->
    56→            when (event) {
    57→                LensDetailEvent.SaveSuccessful -> navController.popBackStack()
    58→                LensDetailEvent.DeleteSuccessful -> navController.popBackStack()
    59→                LensDetailEvent.ConfirmDiscard -> showDiscardDialog = true
    60→            }
    61→        }
    62→    }
    63→
    64→    val isEditMode = state.id != 0
    65→    val title = if (isEditMode) "Edit Lens" else "New Lens"
    66→
    67→    val navigateBack: () -> Unit = {
    68→        if (!state.isDirty) navController.popBackStack()
    69→        else vm.onBackPressed()
    70→    }
    71→
    72→    Scaffold(
    73→        topBar = {
    74→            TopAppBar(
    75→                title = { Text(title) },
    76→                navigationIcon = {
    77→                    IconButton(onClick = navigateBack) {
    78→                        Icon(Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.ArrowBack, contentDescription = "Back")
    79→                    }
    80→                },
    81→                actions = {
    82→                    if (isEditMode) {
    83→                        IconButton(onClick = { showDeleteDialog = true }) {
    84→                            Icon(Icons.Default.Delete, contentDescription = "Delete lens")
    85→                        }
    86→                    }
    87→                    TextButton(
    88→                        onClick = vm::onSaveTapped,
    89→                        enabled = !state.isSaving,
    90→                    ) {
    91→                        Text("Save")
    92→                    }
    93→                },
    94→            )
    95→        },
    96→    ) { innerPadding ->
    97→
    98→        if (state.isLoading) {
    99→            Box(
   100→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize().padding(innerPadding),
   101→                contentAlignment = Alignment.Center,
   102→            ) {
   103→                CircularProgressIndicator()
   104→            }
   105→            return@Scaffold
   106→        }
   107→
   108→        Column(
   109→            modifier = Modifier
   110→                .fillMaxSize()
   111→                .padding(innerPadding)
   112→                .verticalScroll(rememberScrollState())
   113→                .padding(horizontal = 16.dp, vertical = 8.dp),
   114→        ) {
   115→            // Name
   116→            OutlinedTextField(
   117→                value = state.name,
   118→                onValueChange = vm::onNameChanged,
   119→                label = { Text("Name *") },
   120→                placeholder = { Text("e.g. 50mm f/1.4 Summilux") },
   121→                isError = state.nameError != null,
   122→                supportingText = state.nameError?.let { { Text(it) } },
   123→                singleLine = true,
   124→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().padding(vertical = 4.dp),
   125→            )
   126→
   127→            // Make
   128→            OutlinedTextField(
   129→                value = state.make,
   130→                onValueChange = vm::onMakeChanged,
   131→                label = { Text("Make *") },
   132→                placeholder = { Text("e.g. Leitz") },
   133→                isError = state.makeError != null,
   134→                supportingText = state.makeError?.let { { Text(it) } },
   135→                singleLine = true,
   136→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().padding(vertical = 4.dp),
   137→            )
   138→
   139→            // Focal length
   140→            OutlinedTextField(
   141→                value = state.focalLengthMm,
   142→                onValueChange = vm::onFocalLengthChanged,
   143→                label = { Text("Focal length (mm) *") },
   144→                placeholder = { Text("e.g. 50") },
   145→                isError = state.focalLengthError != null,
   146→                supportingText = state.focalLengthError?.let { { Text(it) } },
   147→                singleLine = true,
   148→                keyboardOptions = KeyboardOptions(keyboardType = KeyboardType.Number),
   149→                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxWidth().padding(vertical = 4.dp),
   150→            )
   151→
   152→            // Mount type — autocomplete from existing lens mounts
   153→            MountTypeField(
   154→                value = state.mountType,
   155→                onValueChange = vm::onMountTypeChanged,
   156→                suggestions = state.mountTypeSuggestions,
   157→                isError = state.mountTypeError != null,
   158→                errorMessage = state.mountTypeError,
   159→            )
   160→
   161→            // Max aperture (widest — smallest f-number)
   162→            OutlinedTextField(
   163→                value = state.maxAperture,
   164→                onValueChange = vm::onMaxApertureChanged,
   165→                label = { Text("Maximum aperture *") },

   174→
   175→            // Min aperture (narrowest — largest f-number)
   176→            OutlinedTextField(

   179→                label = { Text("Minimum aperture *") },

   189→            // Aperture increments picker

   246→            confirmButton = {

   249→                }

   254→                }
   255→            },
   256→        )

   259→
   260→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   261→// Shared field composables — reused across gear detail screens
   262→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   263→
   264→/**
   265→ * Mount type text field with type-ahead autocomplete from existing lens mount types.
   266→ * Suggestions are filtered as the user types. The user can enter any free-form value.
   267→ */
   268→@OptIn(ExperimentalMaterial3Api::class)
   269→@Composable
   270→internal fun MountTypeField(
   271→    value: String,
   272→    onValueChange: (String) -> Unit,
   273→    suggestions: List<String>,
   274→    isError: Boolean,
   275→    errorMessage: String?,
   276→    label: String = "Mount type *",
   277→    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
   278→) {
   279→    val filtered = remember(value, suggestions) {
   280→        suggestions.filter { it.contains(value, ignoreCase = true) && it != value }
   281→    }
   282→    var expanded by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
   283→
   284→    ExposedDropdownMenuBox(
   285→        expanded = expanded && filtered.isNotEmpty(),
   286→        onExpandedChange = { expanded = it },
   287→        modifier = modifier.fillMaxWidth().padding(vertical = 4.dp),
   288→    ) {
   289→        OutlinedTextField(
   290→            value = value,
   291→            onValueChange = { onValueChange(it); expanded = true },
   292→            label = { Text(label) },
   293→            placeholder = { Text("e.g. M-mount, EF, F") },
   294→            isError = isError,
   295→            supportingText = errorMessage?.let { { Text(it) } },
   296→            singleLine = true,
   297→            modifier = Modifier
   298→                .fillMaxWidth()
   299→                .menuAnchor(MenuAnchorType.PrimaryEditable, true),
   300→        )
   301→        ExposedDropdownMenu(
   302→            expanded = expanded && filtered.isNotEmpty(),
   303→            onDismissRequest = { expanded = false },
   304→        ) {
   305→            filtered.forEach { suggestion ->
   306→                DropdownMenuItem(
   307→                    text = { Text(suggestion) },
   308→                    onClick = { onValueChange(suggestion); expanded = false },
   309→                    contentPadding = ExposedDropdownMenuDefaults.ItemContentPadding,
   310→                )
   311→            }
   312→        }
   313→    }
   314→}
   315→
   316→/**
   317→ * Read-only dropdown picker for enum values.
   318→ * Used for ApertureIncrements, ShutterIncrements, CameraBodyFormat, etc.
   319→ */
   320→@OptIn(ExperimentalMaterial3Api::class)
   321→@Composable
   322→internal fun <T> EnumDropdown(
   323→    label: String,
   324→    options: List<T>,
   325→    selected: T,
   326→    onSelected: (T) -> Unit,
   327→    displayName: (T) -> String,
   328→    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
   329→) {
   330→    var expanded by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
   331→
   332→    ExposedDropdownMenuBox(
   333→        expanded = expanded,
   334→        onExpandedChange = { expanded = it },
   335→        modifier = modifier.fillMaxWidth().padding(vertical = 4.dp),
   336→    ) {
   337→        OutlinedTextField(
   338→            value = displayName(selected),
   339→            onValueChange = {},
   340→            readOnly = true,
   341→            label = { Text(label) },
   342→            trailingIcon = { ExposedDropdownMenuDefaults.TrailingIcon(expanded = expanded) },
   343→            modifier = Modifier
   344→                .fillMaxWidth()
   345→                .menuAnchor(MenuAnchorType.PrimaryNotEditable, true),
   346→        )
   347→        ExposedDropdownMenu(
   348→            expanded = expanded,
   349→            onDismissRequest = { expanded = false },
   350→        ) {
   351→            options.forEach { option ->
   352→                DropdownMenuItem(
   353→                    text = { Text(displayName(option)) },
   354→                    onClick = { onSelected(option); expanded = false },
   355→                    contentPadding = ExposedDropdownMenuDefaults.ItemContentPadding,
   356→                )
   357→            }
   358→        }
   359→    }
   360→}
   361→
   362→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   363→// Enum display names used in gear detail screens
   364→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   365→
   366→internal val ApertureIncrements.label: String
   367→    get() = when (this) {
   368→        ApertureIncrements.FULL -> "Full stops"
   369→        ApertureIncrements.HALF -> "Half stops"
   370→        ApertureIncrements.THIRD -> "Third stops"
   371→    }
   372→

    21→data class CameraBodyDetailUiState(
    22→    val id: Int = 0,
    23→    val name: String = "",
    24→    val make: String = "",
    25→    val model: String = "",
    26→    val mountType: String = "",
    27→    val format: CameraBodyFormat = CameraBodyFormat.THIRTY_FIVE_MM,
    28→    val shutterIncrements: ShutterIncrements = ShutterIncrements.FULL,
    29→    val notes: String = "",
    30→    // Mount type autocomplete — sourced from existing Lens mount types
    31→    val mountTypeSuggestions: List<String> = emptyList(),
    32→    val isLoading: Boolean = false,
    33→    val isSaving: Boolean = false,
    34→    val isDirty: Boolean = false,
    35→    val nameError: String? = null,
    36→    val makeError: String? = null,
    37→    val modelError: String? = null,
    38→    val mountTypeError: String? = null,
    39→)
    40→
    41→sealed class CameraBodyDetailEvent {
    42→    data object SaveSuccessful : CameraBodyDetailEvent()
    43→    data object DeleteSuccessful : CameraBodyDetailEvent()
    44→    data object ConfirmDiscard : CameraBodyDetailEvent()
    45→}
    46→
    47→class CameraBodyDetailViewModel(
    48→    application: Application,
    49→    savedState: SavedStateHandle,
    50→) : AndroidViewModel(application) {
    51→
    52→    private val bodyId: Int = savedState["id"] ?: 0
    53→    private val gearRepository = GearRepository(AppDatabase.getInstance(application))
    54→
    55→    private val _state = MutableStateFlow(CameraBodyDetailUiState(id = bodyId, isLoading = bodyId != 0))
    56→    val state: StateFlow<CameraBodyDetailUiState> = _state.asStateFlow()
    57→
    58→    private val _events = Channel<CameraBodyDetailEvent>(Channel.BUFFERED)
    59→    val events: Flow<CameraBodyDetailEvent> = _events.receiveAsFlow()
    60→
    61→    init {
    62→        loadMountTypeSuggestions()
    63→        if (bodyId != 0) loadExistingBody()
    64→    }
    65→
    66→    private fun loadMountTypeSuggestions() = viewModelScope.launch {
    67→        // Lens is the vocabulary source for mount types; bodies pick from existing lens mounts.
    68→        gearRepository.getDistinctMountTypes().collect { types ->
    69→            _state.update { it.copy(mountTypeSuggestions = types) }
    70→        }
    71→    }
    72→
    73→    private fun loadExistingBody() = viewModelScope.launch {
    74→        gearRepository.getCameraBodyById(bodyId).collect { body ->
    75→            _state.update {
    76→                it.copy(
    77→                    name = body.name,
    78→                    make = body.make,
    79→                    model = body.model,
    80→                    mountType = body.mountType,
    81→                    format = body.format,
    82→                    shutterIncrements = body.shutterIncrements,
    83→                    notes = body.notes ?: "",
    84→                    isLoading = false,
    85→                    isDirty = false,
    86→                )
    87→            }
    88→            return@collect
    89→        }
    90→    }
    91→
    92→    fun onNameChanged(value: String) =
    93→        _state.update { it.copy(name = value, isDirty = true, nameError = null) }
    94→
    95→    fun onMakeChanged(value: String) =
    96→        _state.update { it.copy(make = value, isDirty = true, makeError = null) }
    97→
    98→    fun onModelChanged(value: String) =
    99→        _state.update { it.copy(model = value, isDirty = true, modelError = null) }
   100→
   101→    fun onMountTypeChanged(value: String) =
   102→        _state.update { it.copy(mountType = value, isDirty = true, mountTypeError = null) }
   103→
   104→    fun onFormatChanged(value: CameraBodyFormat) =
   105→        _state.update { it.copy(format = value, isDirty = true) }
   106→
   107→    fun onShutterIncrementsChanged(value: ShutterIncrements) =
   108→        _state.update { it.copy(shutterIncrements = value, isDirty = true) }
   109→
   110→    fun onNotesChanged(value: String) =
   111→        _state.update { it.copy(notes = value, isDirty = true) }
   112→
   113→    fun onSaveTapped() = viewModelScope.launch {
   114→        val s = _state.value
   115→        if (!validate(s)) return@launch
   116→
   117→        _state.update { it.copy(isSaving = true) }
   118→
   119→        val body = CameraBody(
   120→            id = bodyId,
   121→            name = s.name.trim(),
   122→            make = s.make.trim(),
   123→            model = s.model.trim(),
   124→            mountType = s.mountType.trim(),
   125→            format = s.format,
   126→            shutterIncrements = s.shutterIncrements,
   127→            notes = s.notes.trim().ifBlank { null },
   128→        )
   129→
   130→        if (bodyId == 0) gearRepository.insertCameraBody(body)
   131→        else gearRepository.updateCameraBody(body)
   132→
   133→        _state.update { it.copy(isSaving = false, isDirty = false) }
   134→        _events.send(CameraBodyDetailEvent.SaveSuccessful)
   135→    }
   136→
   137→    fun onDeleteConfirmed() = viewModelScope.launch {
   138→        if (bodyId == 0) return@launch
   139→        val s = _state.value
   140→        val body = CameraBody(
   141→            id = bodyId,
   142→            name = s.name,
   143→            make = s.make,
   144→            model = s.model,
   145→            mountType = s.mountType,
   146→            format = s.format,
   147→            shutterIncrements = s.shutterIncrements,
   148→        )
   149→        gearRepository.deleteCameraBody(body)
   150→        _events.send(CameraBodyDetailEvent.DeleteSuccessful)
   151→    }
   152→
   153→    fun onBackPressed() = viewModelScope.launch {
   154→        if (_state.value.isDirty) _events.send(CameraBodyDetailEvent.ConfirmDiscard)
   155→    }
   156→
   157→    private fun validate(s: CameraBodyDetailUiState): Boolean {
   158→        var valid = true
   159→        if (s.name.isBlank()) {
   160→            _state.update { it.copy(nameError = "Name is required") }
   161→            valid = false
   162→        }
   163→        if (s.make.isBlank()) {
   164→            _state.update { it.copy(makeError = "Make is required") }
   165→            valid = false
   166→        }
   167→        if (s.model.isBlank()) {
   168→            _state.update { it.copy(modelError = "Model is required") }
   169→            valid = false
   170→        }
   171→        if (s.mountType.isBlank()) {
   172→            _state.update { it.copy(mountTypeError = "Mount type is required") }
   173→            valid = false
   174→        }
   175→        return valid
   176→    }
   177→}

   164→            valid = false
   165→        }
   166→        // evReduction is optional (blank = null); if provided it must be a positive number
   167→        val ev = s.evReduction
   168→        if (ev.isNotBlank()) {
   169→            val f = ev.toFloatOrNull()
   170→            if (f == null || f <= 0f) {
   171→                _state.update { it.copy(evReductionError = "EV reduction must be a positive number (e.g. 3.0)") }
   172→                valid = false
   173→            }
   174→        }
   175→        return valid
   176→    }
   177→}
   178→

    45→
    46→class FilmStockDetailViewModel(

    49→) : AndroidViewModel(application) {
    50→
    51→    private val filmStockId: Int = savedState["id"] ?: 0
    52→    private val gearRepository = GearRepository(AppDatabase.getInstance(application))
    53→
    54→    private val _state = MutableStateFlow(
    55→        FilmStockDetailUiState(id = filmStockId, isLoading = filmStockId != 0)
    56→    )
    57→    val state: StateFlow<FilmStockDetailUiState> = _state.asStateFlow()
    58→
    59→    private val _events = Channel<FilmStockDetailEvent>(Channel.BUFFERED)
    60→    val events: Flow<FilmStockDetailEvent> = _events.receiveAsFlow()
    61→
    62→    init {
    63→        if (filmStockId != 0) loadExistingFilmStock()
    64→    }
    65→
    66→    private fun loadExistingFilmStock() = viewModelScope.launch {
    67→        gearRepository.getFilmStockById(filmStockId).collect { stock ->
    68→            _state.update {
    69→                it.copy(
    70→                    name = stock.name,
    71→                    make = stock.make,

    73→                    format = stock.format,
    74→                    defaultFrameCount = stock.defaultFrameCount.toString(),
    75→                    colorType = stock.colorType,
    76→                    discontinued = stock.discontinued,
    77→                    notes = stock.notes ?: "",
    78→                    isLoading = false,
    79→                    isDirty = false,
    80→                )
    81→            }
    82→            return@collect
    83→        }
    84→    }
    85→
    86→    fun onNameChanged(value: String) =
    87→        _state.update { it.copy(name = value, isDirty = true, nameError = null) }
    88→
    89→    fun onMakeChanged(value: String) =
    90→        _state.update { it.copy(make = value, isDirty = true, makeError = null) }
    91→
    92→    fun onIsoChanged(value: String) =
    93→        _state.update { it.copy(iso = value, isDirty = true, isoError = null) }
    94→
    95→    fun onFormatChanged(value: FilmFormat) =
    96→        _state.update { it.copy(format = value, isDirty = true) }
    97→
    98→    fun onDefaultFrameCountChanged(value: String) =
    99→        _state.update { it.copy(defaultFrameCount = value, isDirty = true, defaultFrameCountError = null) }
   100→
   101→    fun onColorTypeChanged(value: ColorType) =
   102→        _state.update { it.copy(colorType = value, isDirty = true) }
   103→
   104→    fun onDiscontinuedChanged(value: Boolean) =
   105→        _state.update { it.copy(discontinued = value, isDirty = true) }
   106→
   107→    fun onNotesChanged(value: String) =
   108→        _state.update { it.copy(notes = value, isDirty = true) }
   109→
   110→    fun onSaveTapped() = viewModelScope.launch {
   111→        val s = _state.value
   112→        if (!validate(s)) return@launch
   113→
   114→        _state.update { it.copy(isSaving = true) }
   115→
   116→        val stock = FilmStock(
   117→            id = filmStockId,
   118→            name = s.name.trim(),
   119→            make = s.make.trim(),
   120→            iso = s.iso.toInt(),
   121→            format = s.format,
   122→            defaultFrameCount = s.defaultFrameCount.toInt(),
   123→            colorType = s.colorType,
   124→            discontinued = s.discontinued,
   125→            notes = s.notes.trim().ifBlank { null },
   126→        )
   127→
   128→        if (filmStockId == 0) gearRepository.insertFilmStock(stock)
   129→        else gearRepository.updateFilmStock(stock)
   130→
   131→        _state.update { it.copy(isSaving = false, isDirty = false) }
   132→        _events.send(FilmStockDetailEvent.SaveSuccessful)
   133→    }
   134→
   135→    fun onDeleteConfirmed() = viewModelScope.launch {
   136→        if (filmStockId == 0) return@launch
   137→        val s = _state.value
   138→        val stock = FilmStock(
   139→            id = filmStockId,
   140→            name = s.name,
   141→            make = s.make,
   142→            iso = s.iso.toIntOrNull() ?: 0,
   143→            format = s.format,
   144→            defaultFrameCount = s.defaultFrameCount.toIntOrNull() ?: 36,
   145→            colorType = s.colorType,
   146→            discontinued = s.discontinued,
   147→        )
   148→        gearRepository.deleteFilmStock(stock)
   149→        _events.send(FilmStockDetailEvent.DeleteSuccessful)
   150→    }
   151→
   152→    fun onBackPressed() = viewModelScope.launch {
   153→        if (_state.value.isDirty) _events.send(FilmStockDetailEvent.ConfirmDiscard)
   154→    }
   155→
   156→    private fun validate(s: FilmStockDetailUiState): Boolean {
   157→        var valid = true
   158→        if (s.name.isBlank()) {
   159→            _state.update { it.copy(nameError = "Name is required") }
   160→            valid = false
   161→        }
   162→        if (s.make.isBlank()) {
   163→            _state.update { it.copy(makeError = "Make is required") }
   164→            valid = false
   165→        }
   166→        val iso = s.iso.toIntOrNull()
   167→        if (iso == null || iso <= 0) {
   168→            _state.update { it.copy(isoError = "ISO must be a positive number") }
   169→            valid = false
   170→        }
   171→        val frames = s.defaultFrameCount.toIntOrNull()
   172→        if (frames == null || frames <= 0) {
   173→            _state.update { it.copy(defaultFrameCountError = "Frame count must be a positive number") }
   174→            valid = false
   175→        }
   176→        return valid
   177→    }
   178→}

1→package com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.gear
     2→
     3→import android.app.Application
     4→import androidx.lifecycle.AndroidViewModel
     5→import androidx.lifecycle.SavedStateHandle
     6→import androidx.lifecycle.viewModelScope
     7→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.AppDatabase
     8→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.CameraBody
     9→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.Filter
    10→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.Kit
    11→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.KitFilter
    12→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.KitLens
    13→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.Lens
    14→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.repository.GearRepository
    15→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.repository.KitRepository
    16→import kotlinx.coroutines.channels.Channel
    17→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.Flow
    18→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.MutableStateFlow
    19→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.StateFlow
    20→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.asStateFlow
    21→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.first
    22→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.receiveAsFlow
    23→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.update
    24→import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
    25→
    26→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    27→// UiState
    28→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29→
    30→/** A lens row in the kit with its primary designation. */
    31→data class KitLensRow(
    32→    val lens: Lens,
    33→    val isPrimary: Boolean,
    34→)
    35→
    36→data class KitDetailUiState(
    37→    val id: Int = 0,
    38→    val name: String = "",
    39→    val cameraBody: CameraBody? = null,
    40→    val lenses: List<KitLensRow> = emptyList(),
    41→    val filters: List<Filter> = emptyList(),
    42→    val notes: String = "",
    43→    // Available items for pickers (populated once cameraBody is chosen)
    44→    val availableBodies: List<CameraBody> = emptyList(),
    45→    val availableLenses: List<Lens> = emptyList(), // filtered by cameraBody.mountType
    46→    val availableFilters: List<Filter> = emptyList(),
    47→    val isLoading: Boolean = false,
    48→    val isSaving: Boolean = false,
    49→    val isDirty: Boolean = false,
    50→    val nameError: String? = null,
    51→    val cameraBodyError: String? = null,
    52→    val lensesError: String? = null,
    53→)
    54→
    55→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    56→// Events
    57→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    58→
    59→sealed class KitDetailEvent {
    60→    data object SaveSuccessful : KitDetailEvent()
    61→    data object DeleteSuccessful : KitDetailEvent()
    62→    data object DuplicateSuccessful : KitDetailEvent()
    63→    data object ConfirmDiscard : KitDetailEvent()
    64→    /**
    65→     * Emitted when the camera body is changed to one with a different mount type, and
    66→     * one or more lenses were removed because they are incompatible.
    67→     * The UI should show the warning from the DESIGN decisions doc.
    68→     */
    69→    data class IncompatibleLensesRemoved(val removedCount: Int) : KitDetailEvent()
    70→}
    71→
    72→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    73→// ViewModel
    74→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→
    76→class KitDetailViewModel(
    77→    application: Application,
    78→    savedState: SavedStateHandle,
    79→) : AndroidViewModel(application) {
    80→
    81→    private val kitId: Int = savedState["id"] ?: 0
    82→    private val db = AppDatabase.getInstance(application)
    83→    private val gearRepository = GearRepository(db)
    84→    private val kitRepository = KitRepository(db)
    85→
    86→    private val _state = MutableStateFlow(KitDetailUiState(id = kitId, isLoading = kitId != 0))
    87→    val state: StateFlow<KitDetailUiState> = _state.asStateFlow()
    88→
    89→    private val _events = Channel<KitDetailEvent>(Channel.BUFFERED)
    90→    val events: Flow<KitDetailEvent> = _events.receiveAsFlow()
    91→
    92→    init {
    93→        loadAvailableBodies()
    94→        loadAvailableFilters()
    95→        if (kitId != 0) loadExistingKit()
    96→    }
    97→
    98→    private fun loadAvailableBodies() = viewModelScope.launch {
    99→        gearRepository.searchCameraBodies("").collect { bodies ->
   100→            _state.update { it.copy(availableBodies = bodies) }
   101→        }
   102→    }
   103→
   104→    private fun loadAvailableFilters() = viewModelScope.launch {
   105→        gearRepository.searchFilters("").collect { filters ->
   106→            _state.update { it.copy(availableFilters = filters) }
   107→        }
   108→    }
   109→
   110→    private fun loadExistingKit() = viewModelScope.launch {
   111→        kitRepository.getKitWithDetails(kitId).collect { kitWithDetails ->
   112→            val lensRows = kitWithDetails.lenses.map { kl ->
   113→                KitLensRow(lens = kl.lens, isPrimary = kl.kitLens.isPrimary)
   114→            }
   115→            // Load available lenses for this kit's mount type
   116→            val mountType = kitWithDetails.cameraBody.mountType
   117→            val compatibleLenses = gearRepository.getLensesByMountType(mountType).first()
   118→
   119→            _state.update {
   120→                it.copy(
   121→                    name = kitWithDetails.kit.name,
   122→                    cameraBody = kitWithDetails.cameraBody,
   123→                    lenses = lensRows,
   124→                    filters = kitWithDetails.filters.map { kf -> kf.filter },
   125→                    notes = kitWithDetails.kit.notes ?: "",
   126→                    availableLenses = compatibleLenses,
   127→                    isLoading = false,
   128→                    isDirty = false,
   129→                )
   130→            }
   131→            return@collect
   132→        }
   133→    }
   134→
   135→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   136→    // Field updates
   137→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   138→
   139→    fun onNameChanged(value: String) =
   140→        _state.update { it.copy(name = value, isDirty = true, nameError = null) }
   141→
   142→    fun onNotesChanged(value: String) =
   143→        _state.update { it.copy(notes = value, isDirty = true) }
   144→
   145→    /**
   146→     * Changes the camera body. Removes any lenses that are incompatible with the new
   147→     * body's mount type. Emits [KitDetailEvent.IncompatibleLensesRemoved] if any were removed
   148→     * so the UI can show the warning from the DESIGN decisions doc.
   149→     */
   150→    fun onCameraBodySelected(body: CameraBody) = viewModelScope.launch {
   151→        val currentLenses = _state.value.lenses
   152→        val compatibleLenses = gearRepository.getLensesByMountType(body.mountType).first()
   153→        val compatibleIds = compatibleLenses.map { it.id }.toSet()
   154→
   155→        val remainingLenses = currentLenses.filter { it.lens.id in compatibleIds }
   156→        val removedCount = currentLenses.size - remainingLenses.size
   157→
   158→        // If the primary lens was removed, auto-promote the first remaining lens
   159→        val hasPrimary = remainingLenses.any { it.isPrimary }
   160→        val adjustedLenses = if (!hasPrimary && remainingLenses.isNotEmpty()) {
   161→            remainingLenses.toMutableList().also { list ->
   162→                list[0] = list[0].copy(isPrimary = true)
   163→            }
   164→        } else {
   165→            remainingLenses
   166→        }
   167→
   168→        _state.update {
   169→            it.copy(
   170→                cameraBody = body,
   171→                lenses = adjustedLenses,
   172→                availableLenses = compatibleLenses,
   173→                isDirty = true,
   174→                cameraBodyError = null,
   175→            )
   176→        }
   177→
   178→        if (removedCount > 0) {
   179→            _events.send(KitDetailEvent.IncompatibleLensesRemoved(removedCount))
   180→        }
   181→    }
   182→
   183→    fun onLensAdded(lens: Lens) {
   184→        val current = _state.value.lenses
   185→        if (current.any { it.lens.id == lens.id }) return // already added
   186→        val isFirst = current.isEmpty()
   187→        _state.update {
   188→            it.copy(
   189→                lenses = current + KitLensRow(lens = lens, isPrimary = isFirst),
   190→                isDirty = true,
   191→                lensesError = null,
   192→            )
   193→        }
   194→    }
   195→
   196→    fun onLensRemoved(lensId: Int) {
   197→        val current = _state.value.lenses.toMutableList()
   198→        val removedWasPrimary = current.find { it.lens.id == lensId }?.isPrimary == true
   199→        current.removeAll { it.lens.id == lensId }
   200→
   201→        // If the primary was removed, auto-promote the first remaining lens
   202→        val adjusted = if (removedWasPrimary && current.isNotEmpty()) {
   203→            current.also { it[0] = it[0].copy(isPrimary = true) }
   204→        } else {
   205→            current
   206→        }
   207→        _state.update { it.copy(lenses = adjusted, isDirty = true) }
   208→    }
   209→
   210→    fun onPrimaryLensChanged(lensId: Int) {
   211→        _state.update {
   212→            it.copy(
   213→                lenses = it.lenses.map { row ->
   214→                    row.copy(isPrimary = row.lens.id == lensId)
   215→                },
   216→                isDirty = true,
   217→            )
   218→        }
   219→    }
   220→
   221→    fun onFilterToggled(filter: Filter) {
   222→        val current = _state.value.filters
   223→        val updated = if (current.any { it.id == filter.id }) {
   224→            current.filterNot { it.id == filter.id }
   225→        } else {
   226→            current + filter
   227→        }
   228→        _state.update { it.copy(filters = updated, isDirty = true) }
   229→    }
   230→
   231→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   232→    // Save
   233→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   234→
   235→    fun onSaveTapped() = viewModelScope.launch {
   236→        val s = _state.value
   237→        if (!validate(s)) return@launch
   238→
   239→        _state.update { it.copy(isSaving = true) }
   240→
   241→        val kit = Kit(
   242→            id = kitId,
   243→            name = s.name.trim(),
   244→            cameraBodyId = s.cameraBody!!.id,
   245→            lastUsedAt = if (kitId == 0) null else
   246→                kitRepository.getKitById(kitId).first().lastUsedAt,
   247→            notes = s.notes.trim().ifBlank { null },
   248→        )
   249→
   250→        val kitLenses = s.lenses.mapIndexed { _, row ->
   251→            KitLens(kitId = kitId, lensId = row.lens.id, isPrimary = row.isPrimary)
   252→        }
   253→        val kitFilters = s.filters.map { filter ->
   254→            KitFilter(kitId = kitId, filterId = filter.id)
   255→        }
   256→
   257→        kitRepository.saveKitWithAssociations(kit, kitLenses, kitFilters)
   258→        _state.update { it.copy(isSaving = false, isDirty = false) }
   259→        _events.send(KitDetailEvent.SaveSuccessful)
   260→    }
   261→
   262→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   263→    // Delete / Duplicate
   264→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   265→
   266→    fun onDeleteConfirmed() = viewModelScope.launch {
   267→        if (kitId == 0) return@launch
   268→        val kit = Kit(id = kitId, name = _state.value.name, cameraBodyId = _state.value.cameraBody?.id ?: 0)
   269→        kitRepository.deleteKit(kit)
   270→        _events.send(KitDetailEvent.DeleteSuccessful)
   271→    }
   272→
   273→    /**
   274→     * Creates a duplicate of this kit with "Copy of [name]" as the default name.
   275→     * Opens the new kit for immediate editing via [KitDetailEvent.DuplicateSuccessful].
   276→     */
   277→    fun onDuplicateTapped() = viewModelScope.launch {
   278→        val s = _state.value
   279→        if (kitId == 0 || s.cameraBody == null) return@launch
   280→
   281→        val copyKit = Kit(
   282→            id = 0,
   283→            name = "Copy of ${s.name.trim()}",
   284→            cameraBodyId = s.cameraBody.id,
   285→        )
   286→        val copyLenses = s.lenses.map { row ->
   287→            KitLens(kitId = 0, lensId = row.lens.id, isPrimary = row.isPrimary)
   288→        }
   289→        val copyFilters = s.filters.map { filter ->
   290→            KitFilter(kitId = 0, filterId = filter.id)
   291→        }
   292→        kitRepository.saveKitWithAssociations(copyKit, copyLenses, copyFilters)
   293→        _events.send(KitDetailEvent.DuplicateSuccessful)
   294→    }
   295→
   296→    fun onBackPressed() = viewModelScope.launch {
   297→        if (_state.value.isDirty) _events.send(KitDetailEvent.ConfirmDiscard)
   298→    }
   299→
   300→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   301→    // Validation
   302→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   303→
   304→    private fun validate(s: KitDetailUiState): Boolean {
   305→        var valid = true
   306→        if (s.name.isBlank()) {
   307→            _state.update { it.copy(nameError = "Kit name is required") }
   308→            valid = false
   309→        }
   310→        if (s.cameraBody == null) {
   311→            _state.update { it.copy(cameraBodyError = "Select a camera body") }
   312→            valid = false
   313→        }
   314→        if (s.lenses.isEmpty()) {
   315→            _state.update { it.copy(lensesError = "At least one lens is required") }
   316→            valid = false
   317→        }
   318→        return valid
   319→    }
   320→}
   321→

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: FrameLog implementation status
description: Full implementation status of the FRAME//LOG Android app — data, VM, and UI layers
type: project
---

## Fully complete layers (compiling cleanly as of 2026-03-19)

**Database / data layer:**
- All 12 Room entities in `data/db/entity/`
- All enums in `Enums.kt`: CameraBodyFormat, FilmFormat, ShutterIncrements, ApertureIncrements, ColorType, RollStatus
- TypeConverters for all enums (string values, not ordinals)
- All 11 DAOs in `data/db/dao/`
- Relation types in `data/db/relation/`: RollWithDetails, FrameWithDetails, KitWithDetails, + join relations
- Export types in `RollExport.kt`
- AppDatabase: singleton, all DAO accessors, 3 multi-DAO @Transaction methods
- All 4 repositories in `data/repository/`

**Infrastructure:**
- `data/AppPreferences.kt` — SharedPreferences wrapper (activeRollId, onboarding, settings, per-roll currentFrame + MRU filters)
- `ui/util/ExposureValues.kt` — shutter speed + aperture value lists, display formatters, EC values
- `ui/util/ExportFormatter.kt` — CSV, JSON, plain text formatters for RollExport

**ViewModel layer (13 VMs):**
GearLibraryViewModel, LensDetailViewModel, CameraBodyDetailViewModel, FilterDetailViewModel,
FilmStockDetailViewModel, KitDetailViewModel, KitSelectorViewModel, RollListViewModel,
RollSetupViewModel, RollJournalViewModel, FrameDetailViewModel, QuickScreenViewModel, SettingsViewModel

---

## UI layer — partially started (session 2026-03-19 evening, interrupted)

**Done — compiling status unknown (not yet verified):**
- `ui/navigation/NavRoutes.kt` — all type-safe @Serializable route definitions (Navigation Compose 2.8.0)
- `ui/navigation/NavGraph.kt` — FrameLogNavGraph NavHost; Gear Library routes wired, others commented as TODO
- `MainActivity.kt` — updated to use FrameLogNavGraph + rememberNavController (placeholder removed)
- `ui/gear/GearLibraryScreen.kt` — full 5-tab implementation: ScrollableTabRow, per-tab search + sort dropdown + LazyColumn, gear cards (LensCard, BodyCard, FilterCard, FilmStockCard, KitCard), SearchAndSortRow generic composable, EmptyState, display name extensions
- `ui/gear/LensDetailScreen.kt` — full form: name, make, focalLength, mountType (autocomplete MountTypeField), maxAperture, minAperture, apertureIncrements (EnumDropdown), filterSize, notes; DiscardChangesDialog, DeleteConfirmDialog; also defines shared internal composables **MountTypeField** and **EnumDropdown** (used by all gear detail screens)

**Not yet started (next session, needs user go-ahead first):**
- `ui/gear/CameraBodyDetailScreen.kt`
- `ui/gear/FilterDetailScreen.kt`
- `ui/gear/FilmStockDetailScreen.kt`
- `ui/gear/KitDetailScreen.kt`
- All Roll screens (RollListScreen, RollSetupScreen, KitSelectorScreen, RollJournalScreen)
- FrameDetailScreen, QuickScreen, SettingsScreen, Onboarding screens

---

## Architecture notes

- No DI framework — repositories get AppDatabase passed directly
- Navigation: type-safe routes via @Serializable data classes; SavedStateHandle auto-populated with route params by Navigation 2.8.0
- ViewModel creation: `viewModel()` inside `composable<T>` — Navigation Compose handles Application + SavedStateHandle injection automatically
- Start destination is GearLibrary temporarily; will move to QuickScreen with onboarding/launch logic later
- MediumFormat excluded from all v1.0 UI (CameraBodyFormat, FilmFormat)
- `collectAsStateWithLifecycle()` used in screen composables (requires lifecycle-runtime-compose; available via lifecycle 2.8.0 BOM)

**Why:** App is offline-first, no server. data/model/ dir not created — entities serve as models directly.
**How to apply:** Wait for explicit go-ahead from user before starting large implementation blocks. Repository layer is the correct access point for ViewModels.

content.text:
**BUILD SUCCESSFUL.** Here's what was fixed and created:

**Errors fixed:**
- `NavGraph.kt` referenced 4 screens that didn't exist — created them all

**Created files:**
- `CameraBodyDetailScreen.kt` — name, make, model, MountTypeField (autocomplete), format dropdown (MEDIUM_FORMAT excluded), shutter increments dropdown
- `FilterDetailScreen.kt` — name, make, FilterTypeField (autocomplete from existing types), EV reduction (optional), filter size (optional)
- `FilmStockDetailScreen.kt` — name, make, ISO, format dropdown, frame count, color type dropdown, discontinued Switch
- `KitDetailScreen.kt` — body picker, add-lens dropdown (mount-type filtered), lens list with RadioButton primary selection and remove, filter checklist, IncompatibleLensesRemoved snackbar

**Collateral fixes:**
- `ColorType.label` was defined `private` in `GearLibraryScreen.kt` and again in `FilmStockDetailScreen.kt` — promoted GearLibraryScreen's to `internal`, removed the duplicate
- `Icons.Default.ContentCopy` / `Icons.Default.Remove` aren't in the core icon set — replaced with `TextButton("Duplicate")` and `Icons.Default.Clear`

All 5 Gear Library detail screens are now complete and compiling. Ready for Roll screens whenever you want to proceed.

# FrameLog session 2026-03-20 morning

## Status at end of session
Gear Library UI complete — all 5 list screens and 5 detail screens building cleanly.

## What was completed
- NavRoutes.kt and NavGraph.kt in place (type-safe @Serializable routes, Navigation Compose 2.8.0)
- GearLibraryScreen.kt — 5-tab ScrollableTabRow (Lenses, Bodies, Filters, Film, Kits)
- LensDetailScreen.kt — includes shared internal composables MountTypeField and EnumDropdown
- CameraBodyDetailScreen.kt
- FilterDetailScreen.kt
- FilmStockDetailScreen.kt
- KitDetailScreen.kt — body picker, lens add/remove/primary-select, filter checklist, snackbar for incompatible lenses removed

## Known issues / environment
- JAVA_HOME needs to be set permanently in Windows environment variables (currently must be set manually each session: JAVA_HOME="C:/Program Files/Android/Android Studio/jbr")

Roll screens in this order:

content.text:
Saved to basic-memory at `framelog/FrameLog session 2026-03-20 morning.md`.

Also noting the JAVA_HOME tip — the quickest permanent fix on Windows 11 is:

```
System Properties → Advanced → Environment Variables → System Variables → New
Variable: JAVA_HOME
Value: C:\Program Files\Android\Android Studio\jbr
```

Then add `%JAVA_HOME%\bin` to the `Path` system variable if it isn't already there. After that, `./gradlew` will find Java without any manual prefix.

# FRAMELOG screen inventory
## Screen Inventory

## Overview

FRAME//LOG contains approximately 26 distinct screens across 5 sections. Most complexity is concentrated in the one-time gear setup layer. Daily field use touches 2-4 screens maximum.

Many screens follow the same list/detail pattern repeated for different entity types — the unique screen template count is closer to 8-10.

See `FRAMELOG_navigation_map.mermaid` for the full navigation diagram.

---

## Onboarding (first run only)

| Screen | Description | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Welcome Screen | App name, tagline, "show me around" / "skip setup" buttons | Sets onboarding boolean flag |
| Add First Lens | Gear library lens edit screen with coach mark overlay | Reuses gear library screen |
| Add First Camera Body | Gear library body edit screen with coach mark overlay | Reuses gear library screen — mount type picker already populated from lens |
| Add First Film Stock | Gear library stock edit screen with coach mark overlay | Reuses gear library screen |
| Load First Roll | Roll setup screen with coach mark overlay | Reuses roll setup screen |
| Widget Setup Instructions | Step-by-step guide to adding 2×4 widget to home screen | Android cannot add widget programmatically. Also accessible from Settings anytime. |

**Coach mark pattern:** Semi-transparent overlay with highlighted cutout, arrow, step counter, "got it ›" button, and "skip tour" link. User performs real interactions — coach mark does not perform actions on their behalf.

---

## Bottom Navigation — 5 Destinations

| Position | Destination | Layer |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 (left) | Gear Library | 4 |
| 2 | Roll List | 3 |
| 3 (center) | Quick Screen | 2 |
| 4 | Active Roll Journal | 3 |
| 5 (right) | Settings | — |

Quick Screen is center position — primary daily destination, thumb-reachable without looking.

---

## Layer 2 — Quick Screen

Single screen. App home. All interactive elements in bottom 60% for one-handed use.

**Header (tappable when multiple loaded rolls exist)**

| Element | Type | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| "active roll · tap to switch" | Muted label | Only shown when multiple loaded rolls exist |
| Roll name | Tappable, underlined with › | Opens Switch Roll bottom sheet |
| Filter count + EV comp | Read-only, right-aligned | Status indicators |
| Frame counter | Read-only | Current / total |

**Switch Roll Bottom Sheet** — appears on header tap. Shows all loaded rolls with radio buttons, frame progress bars, and "last shot" timestamp. No confirmation needed — selection is immediately reversible.

**Interactive fields (top to bottom — frequency order)**

| Element | Type | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Lens selector | Tap to cycle | Cycles through roll's configured lenses |
| Filter chips (up to 4 MRU) + EV sum + picker | Tap to toggle / + to open picker | MRU = most recently used. EV sum right-aligned, ~ prefix if any filter has null evReduction |
| Exposure compensation | Centered stepper | ±3 EV in 1/3 stop increments, nullable |
| Frame pointer | Centered stepper | Manual navigation between frame slots |
| Aperture | Large centered stepper | Bounded by lens min/max, increments from lens |
| Shutter speed | Large centered stepper | Increments from camera body. Whole seconds in distinct color. |
| Note | Text field + mic button | Optional, speech to text available |
| Log Frame | Primary action button | Full width, anchored to bottom |

All fields pre-populate from last logged frame. Overwriting a previously logged frame triggers confirmation prompt.

---

## Layer 3 — Roll Management

### Roll List Screen

Entry point for all rolls. FAB (+) bottom right to create a new roll.

**Tabs:** Active / Finished / Archived (swipe to navigate). Tab shows count when inside (e.g. "Active (3)").

**Active tab sections:**

*   Loaded rolls (camera icon, solid border)
*   Unloaded rolls (canister icon, dashed border, muted, "← swipe to load →" hint)

**Card interactions:**

*   Tap → Roll Journal View
*   Swipe left or right → Load Roll confirmation sheet (unloaded rolls only)
*   Long press → context-aware quick action menu (options vary by roll state)

**Long press menu by state:**

| Roll State | Available Actions |
| --- | --- |
| Active, unloaded | Load Roll (→ confirmation), Open Journal, Delete (→ danger) |
| Active, loaded | Open Journal, Delete (→ danger) |
| Finished | Open Journal, Archive (→ confirmation), Delete (→ danger) |
| Archived | Open Journal, Unarchive (→ confirmation), Delete (→ danger) |

### Roll Setup Screen

Single scrollable form. Two-handed, unhurried. Accessed via FAB on Roll List and during onboarding.

| Section | Fields |
| --- | --- |
| Film stock | Select from library or create new |
|  | Frame count (defaults from stock, overridable) |
|  | Push/pull actuator (Pull 3 → Box → Push 3, ISO updates live) |
|  | Custom ISO override (collapsed link below actuator) |
|  | Film expiry date (optional) |
| Kit (optional) | Load from kit button — pre-fills body, lenses, filters. All editable after. |
| Camera body | Select from library |
| Lenses | Primary lens (required), additional lenses optional. Filtered by mount type. |
| Filters | Chip pattern, optional |
| Details | GPS capture toggle |
|  | Roll name (auto-generated, editable) |
| Actions | Create Roll (unloaded) / Create & Load Roll (loaded) |

### Kit Selector Screen

Accessed from Roll Setup. Searchable list of kits. Read-only selection surface.

*   Tap kit → pre-populates roll setup, returns to form
*   FAB → create new kit (navigates to Kit Detail, returns on save)
*   "Skip — fill gear manually" button at bottom

### Roll Journal View

Scrollable list of all frame slots for a specific roll.

**Header:** Roll name, back arrow (←), overflow menu (⋯), progress bar, film stock + body + ISO info.

**Frame cards — hybrid pattern:**

*   Logged: filled circle + frame number + solid border + aperture, shutter, lens, filters, timestamp, GPS indicator, note preview
*   Unlogged: empty circle + dashed border + "— unlogged —"
*   Current frame: heavier border + "current" label
*   Extra frames labeled "(extra)"

**Bottom bar — state dependent:**

| Roll State | Bottom Bar |
| --- | --- |
| Active, unloaded | Load Roll (primary) + Export |
| Active, loaded | Finish Roll (primary) + Export |
| Finished | Export only |
| Archived | Export only |

**Overflow menu (⋯):** Archive / Unarchive (state dependent), Delete (→ danger confirmation always).

**Roll status transitions:**

*   Active (unloaded) → Active (loaded): Load Roll — confirmation required
*   Active (loaded) → Finished: Finish Roll — confirmation required, irreversible
*   Finished → Archived: Archive — confirmation required
*   Archived → Finished: Unarchive — confirmation required
*   Any state → Deleted: Delete — danger confirmation, permanent

### Frame Detail / Edit Screen

Same fields as Quick Screen (minus frame pointer), pre-populated. Always editable.

*   `isLogged` toggle — marks frame as logged without fabricating a timestamp (retroactive correction)
*   GPS coordinates displayed as tappable link — opens user's preferred maps app via Android geo intent
*   Note field slightly taller than Quick Screen — unhurried context
*   Save Changes button (no auto-advance)
*   Back arrow triggers save prompt if unsaved changes exist
*   Overflow menu (⋯): no additional actions in v1.0

---

## Layer 4 — Gear Library

Five tabs, identical list/detail pattern. All list screens: search bar + sort dropdown, scrollable cards, FAB.

| Tab | Entity | Sort Options |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Lenses | Lens | A–Z, Last Used, Recently Added, Mount Type |
| Bodies | CameraBody | A–Z, Last Used, Recently Added |
| Filters | Filter | A–Z, Last Used, Recently Added, Filter Size |
| Film Stocks | FilmStock | A–Z, Last Used, Recently Added |
| Kits | Kit | A–Z, Last Used, Recently Added |

**Design principle:** All list screens follow the same pattern — search bar + sort, scrollable results, FAB to add, tap to detail. No exceptions in v1.0.

**Gear Detail / Edit screens:** Full CRUD. Delete and Duplicate in overflow menu (⋯) only. Input patterns: `f/` prefix for aperture values, `mm` suffix for filter size, autocomplete for folksonomy fields (mount type, filter type).

**Kit Detail / Edit:** Radio buttons for primary lens designation. Camera body change automatically removes incompatible lenses with warning to review filters.

---

## Settings

| Section | Screen / Setting | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Shooting Defaults | Extra frames per roll | Integer, default 2 |
|  | GPS precision | High / battery saver |
|  | Default export format | CSV / JSON / plain text |
| Appearance | App theme | Light / dark / system |
|  | Accessible color mode | Off by default — alternative indicators for color-coded elements |
| Data & Backup | Export backup | Writes .framelog file via share sheet |
|  | Restore from backup | Destructive — requires confirmation |
| Widget | Widget setup instructions | Same screen as onboarding step 6 |
| Onboarding | Re-run introduction | Resets onboarding boolean flag |
| Support | Tip jar | Ko-fi link or Play in-app purchase |
| About | Version | Read-only |
|  | Privacy policy | External link |
|  | Open source licenses | Generated by OSS Licenses plugin |

---

## Export Flow

Triggered from Roll Journal View. Bottom sheet — not a full screen.

| Format | v1.0 |
| --- | --- |
| CSV | Yes |
| JSON | Yes |
| Plain text | Yes |
| Google Sheets | v1.1 |
| ExifTool command file | v1.1 |

Format selection via radio buttons. Export button hands file to Android share sheet — user chooses destination (Drive, email, Files, etc.). No direct cloud integration in v1.0.

---

## Screen Count Summary

| Section | Screens |
| --- | --- |
| Onboarding | 6 (4 reuse existing screens) |
| Quick Screen | 1 + Switch Roll bottom sheet |
| Roll Management | 5 (Roll List, Roll Setup, Kit Selector, Roll Journal, Frame Detail) |
| Gear Library | 10 (5 list + 5 detail/edit) |
| Settings | 1 (with sub-sections) |
| Export | 1 (bottom sheet) |
| **Total unique screens** | **~24 + 2 bottom sheets** |

1→# 05 roll
     2→## Entity — Roll
     3→
     4→## Fields
     5→
     6→| Field | Type | Nullable | Notes |
     7→| --- | --- | --- | --- |
     8→| id | integer | no | auto-generated primary key |
     9→| name | string | no | auto-generated from film stock + date, user editable |
    10→| filmStockId | integer | no | foreign key → FilmStock |
    11→| cameraBodyId | integer | no | foreign key → CameraBody |
    12→| pushPull | integer | no | default 0 (box speed). Range -3 to +3. -1 = pull 1 stop, +1 = push 1 stop. |
    13→| ratedISO | integer | no | derived from FilmStock.iso + pushPull math. Custom override available. |
    14→| filmExpiryDate | date | yes | expiry date of the specific physical roll loaded |
    15→| totalExposures | integer | no | locked at creation — see calculation note below |
    16→| isLoaded | boolean | no | default false. True = roll is in a camera and drives widget/quick screen. |
    17→| gpsEnabled | boolean | no | default false — if true, lat/lng captured on each frame log |
    18→| status | enum | no | `active \| finished \| archived` |
    19→| loadedAt | timestamp | no | when the roll was created |
    20→| finishedAt | timestamp | yes | when the roll was marked finished — null until then |
    21→| lastExportedAt | timestamp | yes | when the roll was last exported — null until first export |
    22→| notes | string | yes | free text |
    23→
    24→## totalExposures Calculation
    25→
    26→Determined at roll creation, immutable after that.
    27→
    28→*   **Standard body:** `defaultFrameCount + extraFrames` (extraFrames from app setting, default 2)
    29→*   **Half-frame body:** `(defaultFrameCount + extraFrames) × 2`
    30→
    31→Example: 36-exposure roll, +2 extra frames, half-frame body → `(36 + 2) × 2 = 76 slots`
    32→
    33→## Push/Pull and ratedISO
    34→
    35→*   `pushPull` is the authoritative field. Range -3 to +3, default 0.
    36→*   `ratedISO` is derived: `FilmStock.iso × 2^pushPull`. Example: HP5 (ISO 400) pushed 1 stop → ratedISO 800.
    37→*   Custom ISO override: user can manually set ratedISO to a non-standard value (e.g. 350). In this case pushPull is set to null to indicate a custom rating.
    38→*   Display: pushPull = 0 shows "box speed", pushPull = 1 shows "push 1 · ISO 800", custom shows just the ISO value.
    39→
    40→## Roll Status and Loaded State
    41→
    42→*   `status` and `isLoaded` are independent fields.
    43→*   A roll can be `status = active` and `isLoaded = false` (purchased, prepared, sitting in inventory).
    44→*   A roll can be `status = active` and `isLoaded = true` (in a camera, actively shooting).
    45→*   Multiple rolls can have `status = active` simultaneously — one per camera body is the typical case.
    46→*   Multiple rolls can have `isLoaded = true` simultaneously — one per camera body.
    47→*   Only one roll is the "active roll" for the widget and quick screen at any time — selected by the user via the Quick Screen header switcher.
    48→
    49→## Roll Creation Actions
    50→
    51→*   **Create Roll** — sets `status = active`, `isLoaded = false`. Roll appears in inventory but does not drive widget.
    52→*   **Create & Load Roll** — sets `status = active`, `isLoaded = true`. Roll is immediately the active roll for widget and quick screen (if no other loaded roll was already selected).
    53→
    54→## Notes
    55→
    56→*   All Frame slots are created in bulk at roll creation — `frameNumber` 1 through `totalExposures`. No frames are added or removed after creation.
    57→*   `filmExpiryDate` is for the physical roll, not the stock definition. Multiple rolls of the same stock can have different expiry dates.
    58→*   `gpsEnabled` is set at roll creation. If enabled, the app checks for location permissions and requests them via the standard Android permission flow if not already granted. If denied, `gpsEnabled` is set back to false gracefully.
    59→*   `lastExportedAt` is updated after any successful export. Not part of a transaction — failure is non-critical.
    60→
    61→## Relationships
    62→
    63→*   One Roll → one FilmStock
    64→*   One Roll → one CameraBody
    65→*   One Roll → many RollLens (one isPrimary)
    66→*   One Roll → many RollFilter
    67→*   One Roll → many Frames

# 05 roll
## Entity — Roll

## Fields

| Field | Type | Nullable | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| id | integer | no | auto-generated primary key |
| name | string | no | auto-generated from film stock + date, user editable |
| filmStockId | integer | no | foreign key → FilmStock |
| cameraBodyId | integer | no | foreign key → CameraBody |
| pushPull | integer | no | default 0 (box speed). Range -3 to +3. -1 = pull 1 stop, +1 = push 1 stop. |
| ratedISO | integer | no | derived from FilmStock.iso + pushPull math. Custom override available. |
| filmExpiryDate | date | yes | expiry date of the specific physical roll loaded |
| totalExposures | integer | no | locked at creation — see calculation note below |
| isLoaded | boolean | no | default false. True = roll is in a camera and drives widget/quick screen. |
| gpsEnabled | boolean | no | default false — if true, lat/lng captured on each frame log |
| status | enum | no | `active \| finished \| archived` |
| loadedAt | timestamp | no | when the roll was created |
| finishedAt | timestamp | yes | when the roll was marked finished — null until then |
| lastExportedAt | timestamp | yes | when the roll was last exported — null until first export |
| notes | string | yes | free text |

## totalExposures Calculation

Determined at roll creation, immutable after that.

*   **Standard body:** `defaultFrameCount + extraFrames` (extraFrames from app setting, default 2)
*   **Half-frame body:** `(defaultFrameCount + extraFrames) × 2`

Example: 36-exposure roll, +2 extra frames, half-frame body → `(36 + 2) × 2 = 76 slots`

## Push/Pull and ratedISO

*   `pushPull` is the authoritative field. Range -3 to +3, default 0.
*   `ratedISO` is derived: `FilmStock.iso × 2^pushPull`. Example: HP5 (ISO 400) pushed 1 stop → ratedISO 800.
*   Custom ISO override: user can manually set ratedISO to a non-standard value (e.g. 350). In this case pushPull is set to null to indicate a custom rating.
*   Display: pushPull = 0 shows "box speed", pushPull = 1 shows "push 1 · ISO 800", custom shows just the ISO value.

## Roll Status and Loaded State

*   `status` and `isLoaded` are independent fields.
*   A roll can be `status = active` and `isLoaded = false` (purchased, prepared, sitting in inventory).
*   A roll can be `status = active` and `isLoaded = true` (in a camera, actively shooting).
*   Multiple rolls can have `status = active` simultaneously — one per camera body is the typical case.
*   Multiple rolls can have `isLoaded = true` simultaneously — one per camera body.
*   Only one roll is the "active roll" for the widget and quick screen at any time — selected by the user via the Quick Screen header switcher.

## Roll Creation Actions

*   **Create Roll** — sets `status = active`, `isLoaded = false`. Roll appears in inventory but does not drive widget.
*   **Create & Load Roll** — sets `status = active`, `isLoaded = true`. Roll is immediately the active roll for widget and quick screen (if no other loaded roll was already selected).

## Notes

*   All Frame slots are created in bulk at roll creation — `frameNumber` 1 through `totalExposures`. No frames are added or removed after creation.
*   `filmExpiryDate` is for the physical roll, not the stock definition. Multiple rolls of the same stock can have different expiry dates.
*   `gpsEnabled` is set at roll creation. If enabled, the app checks for location permissions and requests them via the standard Android permission flow if not already granted. If denied, `gpsEnabled` is set back to false gracefully.
*   `lastExportedAt` is updated after any successful export. Not part of a transaction — failure is non-critical.

## Relationships

*   One Roll → one FilmStock
*   One Roll → one CameraBody
*   One Roll → many RollLens (one isPrimary)
*   One Roll → many RollFilter
*   One Roll → many Frames

     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## Haptic Feedback
     9→

    11→

    13→*   `−` (decrease) — double short pulse (`VibrationEffect.createWaveform`)
    14→*   Log Frame button — distinct longer confirmation haptic on successful frame log
    15→
